title_001: ADHD Tendency Test — How’s your focus right now?
html_003: Being scattered isn’t about being lazy —
html_004: your brain may simply be built this way
html_006: Let’s take an honest look at where your focus is right now.
html_016: Every answer fills the gauge above in real time.
html_017: There are no right answers. Just picture yourself over the last
html_027: , how often was this true for you?
html_029: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_002: Focus isn’t held by willpower — it’s held by your ‘environment’. Clear distractions out of sight, and keep just one thing visible at a time.
js_004: That extra energy isn’t meant to be killed — it’s meant to flow out. Move your body on a schedule: short walks, stretches, timer breaks.
js_006: The secret is ‘the 3 seconds before it slips out’. Before you decide, buy, or speak, build a rule: wait 3 seconds — or sleep on it one night.
js_008: Keep it all in your head and it all leaks out. Get your to-dos outside — notes, alarms, a calendar — and let the tools remember, not your brain.
js_009: Even when I try to focus on a task, my mind drifts off almost right away.
js_010: I make careless slips (typos, missed details) often enough that I have to double-check.
js_011: I often can’t find where I put things like my keys, wallet, or phone.
js_012: Mid-conversation or in meetings, I lose the thread and think ‘wait, where were we?’
js_013: Boring-but-important stuff (paperwork, tidying) is exactly what I can’t start and keep putting off.
js_014: Anything long to read, I struggle to stay focused all the way through.
js_015: I jump to something new before finishing what I started, leaving things half-open everywhere.
js_016: When someone’s talking, I look like I’m listening but my head is often somewhere else.
js_017: Even sitting still, I fidget with my hands and feet or bounce my leg.
js_018: Situations where I have to sit for a long time feel especially stifling and itchy.
js_019: The thoughts in my head keep spinning and never quiet down.
js_020: Even during downtime, I only feel at ease if I’m doing something.
js_021: I get talkative, or cut in before the other person finishes.
js_022: Waiting in line or at a red light is unbearably boring for me.
js_023: Body or mind, I always feel like the ‘engine is running’.
js_024: Words or actions jump out before I’ve thought them through.
js_025: When I want something, I tend to just buy it on the spot instead of holding back.
js_026: When I feel like doing something, I just start before weighing the consequences.
js_027: When a feeling surges up, I let it out in the moment, all at once.
js_028: When I’m bored, my hand keeps reaching for a hit — phone, videos, snacks.
js_029: I often switch direction on a whim, off from what I’d planned.
js_030: Sometimes I think ‘I shouldn’t say this’ — right after I’ve already said it.
js_031: I get pulled hard toward something new, then lose interest fast.
js_032: I lose track of time, so I’m always racing to make appointments or deadlines.
js_033: When there are several things to do, I freeze, not knowing where to start.
js_034: I make plans just fine — actually following them is the hard part.
js_035: The engine only kicks in once the deadline is right in my face.
js_036: My stuff, papers, and files stay disorganized, so I can’t find them when I need them.
js_037: Things I figured would be ‘quick’ always take way longer than I expected.
js_038: Juggling several things at once, I keep dropping one here and there.
js_046: Right now, your focus and follow-through are mostly running smoothly. Your ADHD tendency came out low. The occasional scatter is a natural wave everyone feels.
js_047: Keep the good rhythm you’ve got — sleep, routines, one thing at a time. On days focus scatters more than usual, start by checking your condition, sleep, and stress.
js_048: Low doesn’t mean ‘none of this applies to me’. If one area’s bar is unusually high, just tending to that single part makes life much easier.
js_051: You’ve got a real swing between focused days and off days. Your ADHD tendency is at a moderate level. You dive deep into things you care about, but stall hard in front of anything boring.
js_052: Don’t white-knuckle it with willpower — build structure. Break big tasks small, run a 25-min focus / 5-min break timer, and write your to-dos where you can see them. These three change the game.
js_053: It’s not that you’re ‘lazy’ — your brain is just weak against boredom. Instead of blaming yourself, add one tool that fits you at a time, and it gets plenty better.
js_056: Signals of inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity showed up clearly overlapping. Your head is always busy, you want to do so much but struggle to finish — and you’ve probably blamed yourself for it a lot.
js_057: Don’t trust your brain — trust your tools. Park your memory outside with alarms, checklists, and a calendar, and use a ‘just start for 2 minutes’ rule to get going. What works differs person to person, so treat it like an experiment.
js_058: This test isn’t a diagnosis — it’s a traffic light. If these traits have weighed on your work, relationships, or peace of mind for a long time, it’s worth getting a proper evaluation from a mental health professional. Asking for help isn’t weakness — it’s strategy.
js_061: Signals came out strong across all four areas. You’ve likely been trying many times harder than the people around you. This isn’t a matter of effort — it’s closer to your brain simply working differently.
js_062: Don’t try to muscle through on willpower alone. Alongside setting up your environment — removing distractions, visual reminders — it’s time to seriously consider professional help. Once you truly understand it, managing yourself gets far easier.
js_063: Don’t panic at a high score. ADHD tendencies come paired with strengths too — creativity, drive, deep hyperfocus. But if daily life has felt heavy for a long time, please do talk to a mental health professional. ☎ 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
js_068: 🌿 You’re in a calm place right now
js_069: ✨ Just noticing is already half the battle
js_070: ⚡ It’s not your fault — it’s just how your brain works
js_071: 🌀 You’ve made it this far — that took real effort

_app.title: ADHD Tendency Test
_app.desc: Being scattered isn’t laziness — a gentle check-in on where your focus is now
